{{Quote|Count Dooku sends his regards. We are at your command. We are designed to disassemble over eleven thousand sentient species and have been specially modified for duty on Jabiim.|Assassin droid Commander A71 to Alto Stratus|<ref name="Star Wars: Republic: The Battle of Jabiim">''[[Star Wars: Republic: The Battle of Jabiim]]''</ref>}}
The '''A-series assassin droid''' was a type of advanced [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] deployed by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. It was one of the more advanced and elite models of combat automaton used by the Separatists, and it should not be confused with the more basic [[battle droid assassin]].

==Characteristics==
At 2.4 meters tall, A-series assassin droids were significantly taller than [[Human/Legends|Humans]], and somewhat resembled [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] in size and physique. They had an angular [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] chassis, and lean faces, dominated by four red [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] beneath a sharp, swept-back crest.<ref name="TNEGtD" />

Although their primary function was the assassination of specific targets, these droids typically fought in sizeable teams, overpowering their targets with firepower and combat skill rather than stealth. They seem to have been used sparingly, and on both their recorded front-line deployments, they were sent against groups of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] at the direct command of the most senior Separatist commanders.

===Weapons===
Assassin droids could use a wide range of weapons. All apparently carried a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] at the left shoulder, but this usually served as a backup to their other armaments. The most basic model supposedly used a hand-held blaster. However, some of the droids on Jabiim carried a fixed blaster under either wrist. Meanwhile the group at [[Phindar/Legends|Phindar]] used a variety of weapons, including a [[Flechette/Legends|flechette]], [[Sonic grenade/Legends|sonic detonator]] launchers, [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]], [[hard-sound gun]]s, hand-held [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors, and, [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]].

==History==
[[File:Confederacy_asssassin_droid.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A-series [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]].]]

Though it was claimed that they were created under the aegis of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], their origin actually predates the organization. The A-series was created by [[Anx/Legends|Anx]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Pollux Poi]], who was hired by the [[Shell Hutts]] to create an assassin droid to attack the [[Gejalli]] [[Kajidic/Legends|kajidic]]. The Gejalli sent [[Dashade/Legends|Dashade]] [[Shadow Killer]]s after Poi, who fled to [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. For twenty years, the A-series served as Poi's bodyguards, stopping the Dashade from killing their creator. After Poi died of natural causes shortly after the conclusion of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the remaining [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]] found a piece of [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] technology which advanced them further. They went to the [[Gree Enclave/Legends|Gree Enclave]], and were hired out as a group of assassins. When [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] traveled to the [[Gree (species)/Legends|Gree]] Enclave, he purchased the droids, knowing that they would be useful in the coming war.

A group of assassin droids was dispatched to the [[Battle of Jabiim]] by Count Dooku, modified for operations on the rain-drenched planet, where they fought alongside the [[Nimbus commando]]s under the direct command of [[Alto Stratus]]. Later in the war, [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] carried eighteen assassin droids aboard her personal [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], [[Last Call (Ginivex-class)|''Last Call'']], which she deployed against [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and his team at [[Phindar Spaceport]]. A number of assassin droids were also attached to the Separatist field headquarters when it was located on [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], but it is not clear whether they were assigned permanently as guards, or if their deployment was a precaution adopted specifically because of Yoda's anticipated arrival.

After the establishment of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], many A-series assassin droids were still serving the Nimbus commandos.<ref name="GaW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Confederacy_asssassin_droid_destrroyed.jpg|200px|thumb|Assassin [[Droid/Legends|droids]] being destroyed by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].]]

In ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'', it is said that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's]] report from Jabiim described the standard model of assassin droid as using a hand-held blaster plus the shoulder-mounted weapon, but no assassin droids with this particular armament are shown in the relevant issues of ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]''.

The units seen in [[Star Wars: Republic: The Battle of Jabiim|''The Battle of Jabiim'']] all appear to carry fixed blasters beneath their wrists, while the only group encountered directly by Anakin, near the end of the battle, appear to be completely unarmed: the mountings for their shoulder cannon are visible, but the weapons themselves are absent.

It is thus not entirely certain whether Anakin's report of the basic assassin droid's weaponry is accurate.

Also, the idea that assassin droids can switch into a fast-moving wheel-like mode reminiscent of the [[Droideka/Legends|droideka]] is only found in the novel, where they are described as having come "spinning" down a corridor before they "unpacked" into combat stances; no indication of such a capability is given in the comic, but the droids' crested heads are somewhat reminiscent of those of the droidekas' [[Colicoid/Legends|Colicoid]] creators, albeit mounted on a humanoid chassis.
